post.description: Generates a new JSON settings backup for all settings areas. If an alternate backup location has been set, the backup is also copied to that location.

text/plain.example: The describing comment to be added to the backup

get.description: Returns a list of JSON configuration backups stored locally, or — if `jsonConfigBackupUSBLocation` is set — a combined list from local storage and the configured USB device.

200.description: List of available JSON configuration backups

example.backup_comment: FPP Settings - Disable Scheduler setting was set to ( 0 ).

get.summary: Get list of JSON backups on device
get.description: Returns a list of JSON configuration files on a specified alternate storage device. Available devices can be obtained from `/backups/devices`, or the currently configured device is stored in the `jsonConfigBackupUSBLocation` setting.

200.description: List of JSON backup filenames on the device

post.description: Restores the specified JSON backup. `Directory` is either `JsonBackups` (local) or `JsonBackupsAlternate` (configured alternate device). `GET /api/backups/configuration/list` can be used to obtain valid directory and filename combinations.

delete.description: Deletes a specific JSON backup. `Directory` is either `JsonBackups` (local) or `JsonBackupsAlternate` (configured alternate device). `GET /api/backups/configuration/list` can be used to obtain valid directories and filenames.

get.description: Downloads a specific JSON backup. `Directory` is either `JsonBackups` (local) or `JsonBackupsAlternate` (configured alternate device). `GET /api/backups/configuration/list` can be used to obtain valid directories and filenames.

200.description: Contents of the specified JSON Settings backup as a download.

404.description: Returned when the requested file cannot be located on disk.

get.description: Returns a list of devices (e.g. USB drives, SSDs) attached to the system that can be used for backups.

post.description: Mounts the specified device to `/mnt/{MountLocation}` (defaults to `/mnt/api_mount`).
Unmounts the drive at `/mnt/{MountLocation}` (defaults to `/mnt/api_mount`).

get.description: Returns a list of full system backup files stored in the local `backups/` directory.

get.summary: Get list of backups on device
get.description: Returns a list of full system backup files stored on the specified device (e.g. a USB drive).

200.description: List of backup directory names on the device

get.description: Returns the cape information for the currently detected hardware cape (from `cape-info` settings).

example.name: K16A-B
example.description: K16A-B is a cape for the BeagleBone Black...

post.description: Signs the cape EEPROM by sending its data to the FalconPlayer.com signing API using the provided `key` and order ID.
Accepts a signed EEPROM data payload and writes it back to the cape EEPROM. Accepts either a multipart file upload (`signingPacket`) or a raw JSON body.

get.description: Returns the cape EEPROM signing data payload for use with an external signing service.
Downloads the cape EEPROM signing data as a binary file attachment.

200.description: EEPROM signing data as binary file attachment

post.description: Redeems a voucher code against the FalconPlayer.com signing API to obtain a signing `key` and order ID.

get.description: Returns a list of available cape EEPROM options for the current platform.
Returns a list of available LED panel cape configuration `key` values.

200.description: Available LED panel cape configuration keys

get.description: Returns the LED panel cape configuration JSON for the specified `key`.
Returns a list of available string cape configuration `key` values.
Returns the string cape configuration JSON for the specified `key`.

delete.description: Resets the E1.31/DDP channel input statistics counters.

get.description: Returns the E1.31 or DDP statistics for inbound packets. Returns a meaningful error if the connection to `fppd` fails.
Returns the current configuration of any output processors.

post.description: Overwrites the output processor settings file with a new configuration and returns the saved configuration.

get.description: Returns the current configuration of the specified output file in JSON format. Common values of `{file}` include `universeOutputs`, `universeInputs`, `co-other`, `dmxInputs`, `co-pwm`, and `co-bbbStrings`. Supports an optional `?ip=` query parameter to fetch from a remote FPP instance.

post.description: Overwrites the specified output configuration file with the `POST` body and returns the saved configuration.

get.description: Returns a list of config files in `/home/fpp/media/config` or an optional subdirectory.
Returns the contents of a specific config file, or a directory listing if the path resolves to a directory.

post.description: Uploads or overwrites a config file in `/home/fpp/media/config`, creating any necessary subdirectories. Accepts a multipart file upload or raw `POST` body.

delete.description: Deletes an empty subdirectory from the specified media directory.

post.description: Creates a subdirectory inside the specified media directory.

get.description: Returns a list of effect (`*.eseq`) files available in the effects directory.
Returns a combined list of all effect (`*.eseq`) files from both the effects directory and the sequences directory.

200.description: Combined list of effect and sequence filenames

post.description: Configures outbound email using the existing settings.
Sends a test email using the existing settings.

get.description: Returns a map of all event (`*.fevt`) files, keyed by event ID (filename without extension).

200.description: Map of all event files keyed by event ID

get.description: Returns the contents of a specific event file. If `{eventId}` is `ids`, returns a map of event IDs to display names.

post.description: Triggers the specified event by sending a `Trigger Event` command to `fppd`.

get.description: Returns plugin-specific file info for the specified file path. The plugin name, extension category, and file path are read from route parameters. The metadata command is defined in the plugin's `pluginInfo.json`.

post.description: Moves the specified file from the `uploads` directory to the correct media subfolder based on its extension, returning a status of `OK` or an error message if not successful.
Notifies any plugin that has registered an `onUpload` handler for the given file extension. `:ext` is the extension category and `**` is the file path.

Handles chunked file uploads via `PATCH` (TUS-style). A `POST` to the same route initiates the session and returns a unique upload ID. Each `PATCH` request delivers a chunk identified by `Upload-Name`, `Upload-Offset`, and `Upload-Length` headers; when all chunks arrive, the file is assembled.

delete.description: Deletes the specified file or directory from a media directory. Validates the resolved path against the allowed base directory to prevent path traversal.

get.description: Downloads the specified file from a media directory.

parameters.name: tail
parameters.description: Return the last N lines instead of the whole file

parameters.name: play
parameters.description: When `1`, set a playback-oriented content type instead of a forced attachment

parameters.name: attach
parameters.description: When `1`, force attachment download for images

200.description: File contents; content type varies by file extension and query params

post.description: Copies the specified file from `:source` to `:dest` within the given directory.
Renames the specified file from `:source` to `:dest` within the given directory.

get.description: Streams the tail of a log file using Server-Sent Events (SSE). Only works for files in the `logs` directory.

parameters.name: lines
parameters.description: Number of existing lines to seed into the stream, from 1 to 500, default 50

text/event-stream.example:
[12-May-2026 01:24:10] NOTICE: fpm is running, pid 80
[12-May-2026 01:24:10] NOTICE: ready to handle connections

text/plain.example: Tail follow is only allowed for log files.

post.description: Uploads a file to the specified media directory.

parameters.name: bs
parameters.description: Block size used for fragmented uploads

parameters.name: sb
parameters.description: Starting block index used for fragmented uploads

get.description: Downloads all files in the specified directory (or comma-separated list of directories) as a zip archive. `logs` and `config` are handled specially to include system log and config files.

200.description: Binary file stream of the compressed system archive.

get.description: Returns a list of files in the specified media directory.

parameters.name: nameOnly
parameters.description: When `1`, return a flat array of filenames instead of the default object envelope

get.description: Returns an array of branches available to switch to, filtering out obsolete version branches and Dependabot branches.
Returns a list of commits present in the `origin` (GitHub) but not in the local repository.

200.description: Commits in origin not yet in local

rows.author: GithubUser1
rows.msg: Use our SaveJsonToString() when generating MQTT warnings JSON message.

rows.author: GithubUser2
rows.msg: Move Playlist initialization into main() via Player::Init()

get.description: Returns lists of `.fppos` files available locally or on GitHub for the current platform. If the `{All}` path parameter is `"all"`, returns all releases regardless of platform.
Returns release asset size information from the GitHub `FalconChristmas/fpp` releases API.

post.description: Discard local changes Performs a hard reset on the current branch, discarding any local changes.

log: HEAD is now at a1b65d43 Git Reset moved - #944
HEAD is now at ebc3abe Fix typo, missing space.

get.description: Returns the status of the local git branch, including any dirty files.

example.log:
On branch master
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/master'.

get.description: Returns a list of media files (includes both music and video files).
Returns the duration of a media item.
Returns metadata streams, codecs, profiles, type for a specific media file.

streams.codec_long_name: H.264 / AVC / MPEG-4 AVC / MPEG-4 part 10

get.description: Returns the current DNS configuration. If not configured, `status` will be `Not Configured`.

post.description: Updates the DNS configuration.

get.description: Returns the currently configured default gateway IP address. May be empty when using DHCP.

post.description: Saves the default gateway IP address to the `gateway` configuration file.

get.description: Returns detailed information about network interfaces, their IP addresses, and Wi-Fi signal strength.

post.description: Creates a new blank DHCP interface configuration file for the specified network interface (e.g. `eth1`, `wlan0`).

get.description: Retrieves the current network interface configuration.

post.description: Updates the saved configuration for the specified `{interface}` but does not restart the network.
Applies the networking settings for the specified `{interface}` at the OS level and restarts the interface.

delete.description: Removes interface persistent names by deleting systemd `.link` files and restoring any USB ethernet adapter config files back to `eth*` names.

post.description: Creates interface persistent names by writing systemd `.link` files that pin each interface's name to its MAC address.

get.description: Returns information about Wi-Fi networks discoverable via the specified `{interface}`. Networks without an SSID may appear in the list.
Returns signal strength information for wireless network interfaces.
Returns the available options for the specified setting. Supports `AudioMixerDevice`, `AudioOutput`, `AudioInput`, and other platform-specific option sets.
Get the playlist named `{PlaylistName}` in FPP JSON format. If `?mergeSubs=1` is specified, sub-playlists are recursively merged into the parent sections.

post.description: Update or Insert (upsert) the playlist named {PlaylistName}.
Start the playlist named `{PlaylistName}`. The optional query parameter `scheduleProtected` (`true`/`false`) prevents the scheduler from stopping this playlist.
Start the playlist named `{PlaylistName}` with repeat mode. The optional query parameter `scheduleProtected` (`true`/`false`) prevents the scheduler from stopping this playlist.

parameters.name: scheduleProtected
parameters.description: Prevent schedule from stopping this playlist

post.description: Start the playlist named `{PlaylistName}` with repeat mode and schedule protection. When `{ScheduleProtected}` is `true`, the scheduler cannot stop this playlist.
Insert an item into the `{SectionName}` section of playlist `{PlaylistName}`.

get.description: Get list of playlist names.

post.description: Insert a new playlist.
Pause the currently running playlist.

get.description: Get a combined list of playlist names and `*.fseq` sequence filenames that are playable.

post.description: Resume a previously paused playlist.
Immediately stop the currently running playlist.
Gracefully stop the currently running playlist.

post.summary: Gracefully stop at end of loop
post.description: Gracefully stop the currently running playlist after completion of the current loop.

get.description: Returns a list of all playlists with any validation errors, total item counts, and total duration.
Get list of installed plugins.

post.description: Install a new plugin. The request body is a `pluginInfo.json` structure with `branch` and `sha` fields added to specify which branch and commit to install.

Server-side proxy for fetching a `pluginInfo.json` from a remote URL. Used to retrieve plugin repository info without CORS issues, and to authenticate against private GitHub repositories using credentials configured on the Developer settings page.

200.description: Plugin info fetched from remote URL

get.description: Returns header indicator data (e.g., notification badges) from all installed plugins that define a `headerIndicators.php` file.
Get `pluginInfo.json` for installed plugin `{RepoName}`. An additional `updatesAvailable` field indicates whether the plugin has commits that have been fetched but not yet merged.
Returns the value of setting `{SettingName}` from plugin `{RepoName}`.

post.description: Sets `{SettingName}` for plugin `{RepoName}` and returns the updated value.
Check plugin `{RepoName}` for available updates by running `git fetch` in the plugin directory and checking for any unmerged commits.
Pull in git updates for plugin `{RepoName}`. Supports an optional `?stream=true` query parameter for streaming output.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: When `true`, stream the upgrade output to the response instead of buffering it

delete.description: Deletes all proxy entries by writing an empty `proxy-config.conf` and triggering an Apache graceful reload.

get.description: Returns the list of IP addresses this FPP instance can proxy.

post.description: Replaces the proxy list with the submitted array of `host`/`description` objects, validates each entry, and triggers an Apache graceful reload.

delete.description: Removes a single IP address from the FPP proxy list.

post.description: Adds a single IP address to the FPP proxy list if it does not already exist.

get.description: Fetches a URL on a remote FPP instance via server-side proxy to avoid CSP restrictions.

post.summary: Proxy a command to remote FPP (v2 — JSON body)
post.description: Proxies a named action to a remote FPP instance by IP address. Supported actions: `listUpgrades`, `reboot`, `restartFppd`, `upgradeOS`.

get.description: Returns the list of known remote FPP systems from `fppd` multiSync discovery.
Returns the current FPP schedule configuration from `schedule.json`.

post.description: Saves the new schedule configuration to `schedule.json`.

text/plain.example: Unable to open schedule.json for writing.

post.description: Sends a reload command to `fppd` to re-read the schedule configuration.

get.description: Returns a list of currently installed scripts.

post.description: Installs a remote script from the script repository.

get.description: Returns the source code of a remote script from the script repository.

text/plain.example: The content of the script as a string

get.description: Returns the source code of an installed script.

text/plain.example: The content of the script as a string

post.description: Writes the `POST` request body to the file specified by `{scriptName}`.
Runs a locally installed script.

text/plain.example: The output of the script as a String

get.description: Returns a list of all `*.fseq` sequence files.

post.description: If the sequence was paused via `sequence/current/togglePause`, steps the sequence forward one frame.
Stops the currently playing sequence. Only valid if the sequence was started via `/api/sequence/{SequenceName}/start/{startSecond}`.
Pauses or resumes the currently playing sequence. Only valid if the sequence was started via `/api/sequence/{SequenceName}/start/{startSecond}`.

get.description: Downloads the `*.fseq` file for the named sequence.

post.description: Uploads a new `*.fseq` sequence file.

get.description: Returns `name`, `version`, `id`, `time`, and other details from the `*.fseq` file for the named sequence.

post.description: Starts the given sequence at the specified time frame. Only intended for testing. In most situations, use the "Start Playlist" command from the command API and pass the sequence name as the playlist name.

get.description: Returns the `settings.json` metadata file as a JSON list of settings.
Get info about a particular setting, including its current `value`.

example.name: AudioFormat
example.tip: The Audio Format generated by the decoder

put.description: Sets the value for a specific setting.
Updates a sub-value held as JSON within a setting's value. Only valid for settings stored as JSON.

text/plain.example: raw json of hierarchy to update

get.description: Returns the statistics file that will be shared with the development team if sharing statistics is enabled. A cached file is returned unless it is more than 2 hours old or `?force=1` is passed, in which case it is regenerated.

post.description: Transmits the statistics payload to the remote stats server configured in the `statsPublishUrl` setting.
Restarts the `fppd` process. Pass `?quick=1` to reload some configuration without a full restart.

parameters.name: quick
parameters.description: When `1`, send a reload signal to a running fppd instead of a full stop/start

post.description: Skips the current boot delay by creating a skip flag file, allowing FPP startup to proceed immediately.
Starts the `fppd` process idempotently (if it isn't already running).
Stops the `fppd` process if it is running.

get.description: Returns basic information about the system.
Returns a list of all installed and available OS package names via `apt list --all-versions`.
Returns description, dependencies, and installation status for the specified OS package.

post.description: Reboots the operating system.

get.description: Returns release notes for the specified FPP version tag from the GitHub releases API.

post.description: Executes a clean shutdown of the operating system.

get.description: Returns `fppd`, network, current playlist, schedule, utilization, host, version, and MQTT status. Pass an optional array of IP addresses (e.g. `&ip[]=192.168.0.1&ip[]=192.168.0.2`) to query remote instances instead.
Returns the current FPP update/upgrade status, including whether a newer version is available, the current commit, and any major version or end-of-life warnings.
Returns the current volume if `fppd` is running, or the `Volume` setting value if not.

post.description: Sets the system volume. The new level should be passed as a JSON body.

get.description: Returns the current Test Mode configuration for this instance.

post.description: Sets the current Test Mode configuration for this instance.

get.description: Returns the current system time as a formatted string.
